title: ballota hispanica subsp. mollissima
intro: Ballota hispanica subsp. mollissima, also known as the sweet woodruff or fragrant woodruff, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is also rich in antioxidants, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Ballota hispanica subsp. mollissima can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
